danger_signals.visual_form: Red triangles on motion sensor display
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile alien creatures or threats nearby; directional awareness of danger
Player vulnerability increasing; imminent death risk if not recharged

danger_signals.visual_form: Depleting oxygen level bar UI element
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard (low-gravity, oxygen-free spaces); lethal threat if not restored

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual renderings of molten material pits, crushing ceilings, narrow dark passages
danger_signals.meaning: Instant-death or high-damage zones; spatial threat communication through level design

danger_signals.visual_form: Motion sensor malfunction or distortion visual feedback
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of tactical awareness; increased danger due to sensor unreliability
Unsolved puzzle or incomplete objective; blocked path indicates unfinished task

safety_signals.visual_form: Green squares on motion sensor display
safety_signals.meaning: Friendly NPCs nearby; reduced immediate danger zones or mission-critical entities

safety_signals.visual_form: Wall-mounted recharge station architecture; visual cues in environment
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for shield/oxygen restoration; proximity to recharge indicates sanctuary

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinctive terminal/device architecture placed in level openings
safety_signals.meaning: Save point location; checkpoint safety; rarity communicates progression stakes
Mission briefing available; narrative guidance; objective clarification reduces uncertainty

safety_signals.visual_form: Rendered spaces with visibility and navigable pathways
safety_signals.meaning: Safer zones compared to dark/narrow passages; reduced environmental threat density

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ship's crest of UESC Marathon (featured on game cover)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Computer terminals with AI interface displays; ship architecture (walls, doors, recharge stations); interior colony ship setting

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct enemy creature models visually differentiated from human/ship elements
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Exterior space setting threat; motion sensor red triangle indicators identify faction allegiance through hostile status

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy creatures spawned throughout levels; hostile indicators on motion sensor; combat-ready environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively attacking; player forced into defensive/offensive gameplay; level objectives tied to eliminating threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Oxygen-free environments; low-gravity areas; magnetic field interference affecting sensor reliability
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must manage oxygen levels; environmental hazards restrict movement; sensor unreliability forces tactical caution

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Locked doors blocking access; inaccessible platforms; sealed areas in level design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must explore non-linear level spaces; requires switch activation; necessitates backtracking and investigation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Recharge stations operational; pattern buffers accessible; functional computer terminals; navigation map visible on HUD
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can save progress; can restore shields/oxygen; can access mission briefings; can rely on motion sensor

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shield bar critically low or depleted; oxygen bar critically low or depleted; visual screen effects indicating damage state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player seeks immediate recharge station; avoids combat; prioritizes survival over exploration

environmental_storytelling.example: Terminals contain engineering documents, crew diaries, and AI conversations that reveal plot without cutscenes; player must actively seek out terminals to understand situation context

environmental_storytelling.technique: Level progression gating via plot terminals
environmental_storytelling.example: Specific terminals must be accessed to teleport to next level; gates advancement on narrative requirements, not just combat completion
Colony ship (Marathon constructed from Mars moon Deimos) setting communicates sci-fi context; interior passages, recharge stations, and sealed areas tell story of functional vessel under siege
Specific level designs (e.g., 'Smells Like Napalm, Tastes Like Chicken') feature both open and maze-like sections communicating combat diversity and exploration challenges
Secret terminals difficult to locate contain designer messages; reward player curiosity and exploration, expanding narrative beyond mandatory path

Some levels require endpoint reach only; others mandate item retrieval, switch activation, area exploration, or creature extermination; objective communicated through terminal briefings and level design constraints

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can explore freely, engage enemies, solve puzzles; full tactical capability
Seeks recharge stations; avoids combat; prioritizes defensive positioning
Must find recharge station or exit hazardous zone; behavior driven by survival necessity
Visible on motion sensor as red triangles; actively attack player; force combat or avoidance behavior
Indicated on motion sensor but not yet engaged; player can plan approach or avoidance strategy
Marked as green squares on motion sensor; indicate mission-critical zones or safe areas
Provides mission briefings, objective clarification, and narrative exposition; gates level progression; communicates strategic information

notes: Marathon (1994) uses a motion sensor HUD element as primary world-state communication tool—red triangles for enemies, green squares for allies creates immediate tactical awareness. The game's world-state manifestation is heavily tied to ship systems integrity: oxygen availability, gravity fields, and magnetic interference directly alter player behavior and capability. Narrative world-state (invasion status, objectives) is communicated through mandatory computer terminal access rather than environmental visual changes, creating a hybrid system where spatial design and UI communication work in tandem. The rarity of save points (pattern buffers) is itself a world-state signal—their scarcity increases tension and stakes perception. Environmental hazards (molten pits, crushing ceilings, narrow passages) communicate danger through pure spatial design without requiring UI explanation.
